summary: Ghost Order Protection (Block 8.1) - Atomic Inventory Lock feature fully verified. Backend API returns 409 with correct message for already-claimed listings, uses atomic find_one_and_update for race condition prevention, and properly rolls back on failures. Frontend correctly handles 409 errors with toast and redirect to /nectar.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Ghost Order Protection feature fully tested. Atomic lock uses find_one_and_update at honeypot.py lines 524-528. 409 handling in frontend at ISOPage.js lines 413-416 and 432-435.

backend_tests.test_checkout_returns_409_for_nonexisten: PASS - Returns 409 with 'This honey has already been claimed!'
backend_tests.test_409_response_contains_exact_message: PASS - Message exactly matches 'This honey has already been claimed!'
backend_tests.test_checkout_endpoint_reachable: PASS - Endpoint returns 409 for invalid listing
backend_tests.test_checkout_with_real_listing_and_roll: PASS - Verified Stripe failure triggers rollback to ACTIVE status with cleared lock fields
backend_tests.test_cancel_order_endpoint_structure: PASS - Returns proper JSON error structure

frontend_code_review.handleBuyNow_409_handling: PASS - Lines 413-416: Catches 409, shows toast 'Buzzkill! Someone else just grabbed this record. Keep hunting!' and navigates to /nectar
frontend_code_review.handleMakeOfferSubmit_409_handling: PASS - Lines 432-435: Same handling as handleBuyNow

code_implementation_review.atomic_find_one_and_update: VERIFIED - honeypot.py line 524: Uses db.listings.find_one_and_update with filter {id: listing_id, status: 'ACTIVE'}
code_implementation_review.lock_fields_set: VERIFIED - honeypot.py line 526: Sets status='PENDING', locked_at, locked_by in atomic operation
code_implementation_review.409_on_null_result: VERIFIED - honeypot.py line 532: Returns 409 'This honey has already been claimed!' when find_one_and_update returns null
code_implementation_review.rollback_on_stripe_failure: VERIFIED - honeypot.py lines 600-602: On Stripe error, rolls back listing to ACTIVE with $unset for locked_at/locked_by
code_implementation_review.cancel_order_clears_locks: VERIFIED - honeypot.py line 982: Cancel order uses $unset to clear locked_at and locked_by

verification_notes.race_condition_prevention: The atomic find_one_and_update ensures that only ONE checkout request can claim a listing. If two requests come in simultaneously, only the first one will succeed in the atomic update; the second will get null back and return 409.
rollback_scenarios: 1. Stripe checkout creation fails → listing rolled back to ACTIVE
2. User tries to buy own listing → listing rolled back to ACTIVE
3. Invalid listing type or missing offer amount → listing rolled back to ACTIVE
4. Seller Stripe not connected → listing rolled back to ACTIVE
5. Order cancelled by seller → listing rolled back to ACTIVE with $unset on lock fields
